response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior individual contributor with genuine distributed-systems depth and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling, but the role is an executive CTO position requiring years of senior technical leadership, a track record scaling engineering organizations from 10 to 30+, and demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems in regulated environments. The resume shows no people-leadership, org-building, or executive experience, and the AI-agent work (MCP code generators and tooling) is infrastructure-adjacent rather than operating stateful multi-agent systems with financial consequences. Fintech exposure exists (RIDE Capital) but is brief and IC-level.
strengths: Deep backend and distributed-systems experience, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second
Recent hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at Naver
Broad polyglot engineering range with strong testing and craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, open-source maintainership)
concerns: No engineering-leadership or team-scaling experience; entire career trajectory is individual contributor
No production multi-agent AI systems operated in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments
Fintech exposure limited to roughly one year as a senior developer, with no credit-risk, payments, or compliance architecture ownership

key_factors.factor: Missing executive and team-scaling leadership track record
No production multi-agent AI systems experience
